Class Workout

java.lang.Object
hr.unizd.workoutlog.model.Workout
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class Workout extends Object implements Serializable
Represents a single workout entry in the WorkoutLog application.

This class is a model — it holds data about one workout session and provides controlled access to that data through getter methods (encapsulation). It implements Serializable so that workout objects can be saved to and loaded from a binary file using Java's built-in serialization mechanism.

See Also:
  • Intensity
  • Serialized Form

  • Constructor Details

    • Workout

      public Workout(String exerciseName, int durationMinutes, String dayOfWeek, Intensity intensity, boolean cardio, boolean strength, String note)
      Creates a new Workout with all required information.
      Parameters:
      exerciseName - name of the exercise (must not be empty)
      durationMinutes - duration in minutes (must be greater than zero)
      dayOfWeek - day of the week (e.g. "Monday")
      intensity - intensity level (Light, Medium, or Hard)
      cardio - true if this workout has a cardio component
      strength - true if this workout has a strength component
      note - optional note; may be an empty string, not null

  • Method Details

    • getExerciseName

      public String getExerciseName()
      Returns the name of the exercise.
      Returns:
      exercise name

    • getDurationMinutes

      public int getDurationMinutes()
      Returns the duration of the workout in minutes.
      Returns:
      duration in minutes

    • getDayOfWeek

      public String getDayOfWeek()
      Returns the day of the week when the workout took place.
      Returns:
      day of the week (e.g. "Monday")

    • getIntensity

      public Intensity getIntensity()
      Returns the intensity level of the workout.
      Returns:
      intensity level
      See Also:
      • Intensity

    • isCardio

      public boolean isCardio()
      Returns whether this workout includes a cardio component.
      Returns:
      true if cardio

    • isStrength

      public boolean isStrength()
      Returns whether this workout includes a strength component.
      Returns:
      true if strength

    • getNote

      public String getNote()
      Returns the optional note attached to this workout.
      Returns:
      note text, or an empty string if no note was provided

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Returns a concise, human-readable summary of this workout.

      This is the text displayed in the JList on the right panel of the application. The format is: 

        Running | Monday | 30 min | Medium | Cardio
      The category part shows "Cardio", "Strength", "Cardio + Strength", or "None" depending on which boxes were checked.
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object
      Returns:
      formatted one-line summary of the workout
